driver: playwright/chromium against dev server :3100 (fresh after a Turbopack-cache crash)
scope: Marek (prospective buyer) × evaluate-whether-to-adopt on public marketing surfaces (/, /cena, /clanek)

findings.title: No proof / social proof anywhere on the marketing path — no results, testimonials, logos, or quantified outcomes
findings.expected: A skeptical buyer needs credible evidence it works (the #2 driver after pricing): quantified results ('cut PNO by X'), customer logos, testimonials, or third-party ratings.
findings.got: Homepage is hero → 3 feature cards → CTA, with only a 'Works across' integration-logo row. No customer proof of any kind; pricing has none either.
findings.code_check: confirmed-absent — BrandLanding.tsx renders Hero, FEATURES (3 cards), closing CTA only. No proof component exists to have missed. (A live demo DOES exist via 'See it work' → /dashboard, but that's a demo, not social proof.)
findings.suggested_acceptance: Add a proof band on the homepage/pricing: a quantified result or two from the case-study data (e.g. PNO/ROAS improvement), and reframe the live dashboard as 'see it on real numbers'.

findings.title: The hero is brand-metaphor ('forges your advertising into one unbreakable monument'); the concrete value lives only in the cards below
findings.expected: Within seconds, a fast-skimming buyer knows what it does and for whom.
findings.got: Headline + subhead are poetry; the real 'what' (AI watches/triages/writes ads across Google Ads/Sklik/Meta/TikTok) is in the 3 cards. It also never states the audience (e-shop vs agency).
findings.code_check: confirmed — BrandLanding.tsx hero copy is metaphor; FEATURES carry the specifics. No audience line.
findings.suggested_acceptance: Lead with one concrete sentence (what + for whom) above the metaphor, or fold a specific into the subhead.

findings.title: 'Systedo' still appears where the product is branded 'Adamant' (pricing page title + footer)
findings.expected: Consistent product name — naming wobble erodes the 'real, polished product' impression for a trust-sensitive buyer.
findings.got: Page title 'Ceník — Systedo · Adamant' and footer '© 2026 — případová studie, nikoli oficiální web Systedo', while the site is branded Adamant throughout.
findings.code_check: not-done (render-confirmed) — same family as the authed-shell 'Systedo' leftover seen in Tomáš's run; the rebrand is incomplete in several metadata/footer spots.
findings.suggested_acceptance: Sweep remaining 'Systedo' strings (titles, footer, metadata) to 'Adamant' (or keep one intentional 'by Systedo' attribution, consistently).

findings.title: The primary CTA 'Start free' routes to /app (an auth wall in prod), not to the genuinely-free public exploration
findings.expected: The loudest button gives a frictionless first look (buyers want to look before logging in).
findings.got: 'Start free' → /app (Google OAuth in prod; there's no free-account concept). The frictionless path ('See it work' → /dashboard, and the public dashboard/kampane/ai-asistent) is the secondary, quieter CTA.
findings.code_check: confirmed — Start free → /app (auth-gated except under DEV_AUTH); See it work → /dashboard (public).
findings.suggested_acceptance: Point 'Start free' at the genuinely-free public exploration (or a real signup), and keep /app for 'Open the app'. Otherwise the biggest CTA dead-ends a prospect at a login.

findings.title: /clanek is a strong example of the tool's output but isn't positioned as proof
findings.expected: Show, don't tell — surface the generated article as evidence of the content capability.
findings.got: A genuinely good full article sits under a plain 'Článek' nav item, never framed as 'see what the AI writes'.
findings.suggested_acceptance: Link to /clanek from the AI-ad-studio card / a proof band as 'an article this tool drafted'.

passed: PRICING IS TRANSPARENT — Free + Pro (490 Kč/měsíc) with clear feature lists and a recommended tier, no 'contact us' wall. This clears the single biggest buyer demand (research: 49% name missing pricing the #1 thing to fix).
The 3 feature cards translate the offering into concrete surfaces (dashboard / campaign intelligence / AI ad studio) with channel coverage (Google Ads, Sklik, Meta, TikTok).
A real, frictionless 'see it work' path exists (the public dashboard/kampane/ai-asistent on live-ish numbers) — a genuine try-before-buy.
Honest about being a case study (Stripe not wired, sample data) rather than faking a checkout.
journeys.scope_note: These surfaces openly disclaim they are a portfolio case study (no Stripe, illustrative data), so a literal *purchase* can't complete — that is the artifact's nature, not a defect. The findings above are the ones that would matter even if it were a real product (proof, clarity, brand consistency, CTA routing).
